examples: 200,000 specimens of fungus are kept at the Komarov Botanical Institute.
These are not single individual plants; each one is a symbiotic combination of an alga and a fungus.
But this particular fungus nibbles away only at certain layers in the wood, leaving other layers alone.
The survivor, a fungus called Muscodor albus, had killed the others by gassing them.
Strobel cautions that fermenting fungus into huge vats of cheap biodiesel is still years away.
